lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, are you busy?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Me? I'm always busy.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: See that man there? That's the professor. If he had his way, I think he'd never let me sleep!

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Anyway, how might I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering what you do here.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Glad you ask. This is the Observatory reception.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Up on the cliff is the Observatory dome, from which you can view the heavens.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Usually...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean, 'usually'?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: *Ahem*. Back to work, please.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I'd speak with the professor. He'll explain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that board for?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: The one behind me? We use it to keep track of fallen stars and those that find them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fallen stars?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: That's right. From time to time, stars fall from the sky and land somewhere in Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Whenever one does, we make a note on the board. If someone finds it, we note their name as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. And how do people find them?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: There's a lot of amateur astronomers out there. Many of them have small telescopes in their homes. They use them to work out when a star is going to fall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I do that?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I imagine so. Just get yourself a telescope and look out for stars.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Awesome. I might just give it a go.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Good luck. If you're interested, I hear there's a dwarf at the Mining Guild who's keen on gathering stardust. If you find some, he might be willing to trade with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good to know. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: The one behind me? We use it to keep track of fallen stars and those that find them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes. I've seen one of those before.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: You're very lucky. They are quite rare. Anyway, whenever one falls, we make a note on the board. If someone finds it, we note their name as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. And how do people find them?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: There's a lot of amateur astronomers out there. Many of them have small telescopes in their homes. They use them to work out when a star is going to fall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I do that?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I imagine so. Just get yourself a telescope and look out for stars.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Awesome. I might just give it a go.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Good luck. If you're interested, I hear there's a dwarf at the Mining Guild who's keen on gathering stardust. If you find some, he might be willing to trade with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good to know. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better be off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just looking around, thanks.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Okay, just don't break anything. If you need any help, let me know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have a look through that telescope?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: You can. You won't see much though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that's because?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Just talk to the professor. He'll fill you in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi assistant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait a minute, what's your real name?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: My real name?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I only know you as the professor's assistant. What's your actual name?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Patrick.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, Patrick, I'm [player name].

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Well, hello again, and thanks for helping out the professor.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Oh, and, believe it or not, his name is Mambo-duna- roona, but don't tell him I told you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You made that up!

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: No, honest! Anyways, you've made my life much easier. Have a drink on me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks very much.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: No problem. Scorpius would be proud.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: You may want to check out the book on the table, and perhaps look around for a grave...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, sorry, I can't carry anything else.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Well, then, please come back when you do.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: How was the wine?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was good stuff, *hic*! Wheresh the professher?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: The professor? He's up in the Observatory. Since the bridge was ruined, you will have to travel through the dungeon under the goblins' settlement.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was good stuff! Where's the professor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look what I have.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Good for you. As much as I want to admire your possessions, I do have work to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, right. Sorry.

sections.title: Using any noted item on him

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of this?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I'd rather you show me the actual item, as opposed to the note form.
